title: webhook.v1
description: The outbound webhook wire shape: the delivery envelope POSTed to a subscriber's URL, plus the signed-header scheme that authenticates it. Derived from the parent meeting-api's real envelope (webhook_delivery.build_envelope) + header builder (build_headers). Both the system hooks (billing/analytics) and per-client hooks (user-configured webhook_url) share this shape.

EventType.description: The webhook event types this contract delivers. meeting.* mirror the meeting FSM (lifecycle.v1); recording.* / transcription.* are post-meeting artifacts.

Envelope.description: The standardized webhook payload envelope. Every delivery — system or per-client — is exactly this shape. The body POSTed to the subscriber is `JSON.stringify(Envelope)`; the HMAC signs `"<timestamp>." + body`.

event_id.description: stable identity of the logical event (`evt_<hex>`); delivery is at-least-once and the receiver dedupes on this key. The SAME logical event carries the SAME event_id across every (re)delivery — see the webhook.v1 README 'Delivery semantics'.

created_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp the envelope was built

data.description: event-type-specific body. For meeting.* events this is `{ meeting: {...}, status_change?: {...} }`; kept open (no additionalProperties lock) so post-meeting artifact events can carry their own shapes.

SignatureHeaders.description: The headers a verifier recomputes to authenticate a delivery. The signature is `sha256=<hex(hmac_sha256(secret, "<X-Webhook-Timestamp>." + raw_body))>`. X-Webhook-Timestamp bounds replay. Present only when the subscriber configured a webhook_secret.

X-Webhook-Timestamp.description: unix seconds the signature was computed at (replay window)

Authorization.description: Bearer <secret> — legacy shared-secret auth, carried alongside the HMAC for back-compat
